sources.writer_profile: Alberto Luchini is one of Spain's most respected food journalists, with decades of criticism for major national outlets. He is a contributing critic at 7 Caníbales, an independent gastronomy magazine that describes itself as a space for journalists to express themselves freely about food and restaurants. His Madrid coverage is dense and well-sourced, focusing on new openings and independent dining.
sources.article_title: Un año de novedades en Madrid

sources.writer_profile: Brenda Alonso (@Alonbren) is a food and culture journalist writing for The Objective, an independent Spanish digital newspaper. She covers Madrid's restaurant scene with a focus on critical recognition, new openings, and the city's evolving gastronomic landscape. Her coverage engages directly with industry guides like Repsol and Michelin while maintaining editorial independence.
sources.article_title: Los 14 restaurantes de Madrid que se estrenan o consiguen más Soles en la Guía Repsol 2025

sources.writer_profile: Paul Richardson is a British food and travel writer based in Spain since 1989. A contributing editor at Condé Nast Traveller US/UK, he also writes for the Financial Times, Daily Telegraph, and Saveur. He is the author of A Late Dinner: Discovering the Food of Spain and lives on a farm in Extremadura. His guide to eating in Madrid reflects over three decades of first-hand knowledge of the city's restaurant scene.
sources.article_title: Paul Richardson's guide to eating in Madrid

sources.writer_profile: Gorka Elorrieta is the food and drink editor of Time Out Madrid, writing in English for the international edition. A Madrid-based editor, he has been covering the city's restaurant scene for Time Out's local editorial team. Note: Time Out operates the Time Out Market Madrid food hall, which creates a structural commercial interest in the broader hospitality sector.
sources.article_title: 30 Best Restaurants in Madrid For 2025, By Local Critics
sources.commercial_conflict_note: Time Out operates Time Out Market Madrid food hall.

sources.writer_profile: Lauren Aloise is a trained chef and food writer who has lived in Madrid since 2009, when she moved from the United States. She founded the Madrid Food Tour in 2012 (now Devour Tours) and completed culinary training at Barcelona's Hofmann Culinary School. Her writing on Spanish Sabores draws on deep local knowledge of the city's eating culture, from traditional taverns to international restaurants. Note: as co-founder of Devour Tours, which recommends restaurants on food tours, a potential commercial interest exists.
sources.article_title: Where to Eat in Madrid in 2025: The Ultimate Food Guide
sources.commercial_conflict_note: Author co-founded Devour Tours, a Madrid food tour operator that recommends restaurants. Spanish Sabores appears editorially independent.

restaurants.name: Barbudo
restaurants.signature_dish: Canelón de faisán, arroces melosos, garbanzos con rabo de toro
restaurants.quote: Taberna y casa de comidas con barra de pintxos y tapas arriba, bistró abajo. Pan tumaca y jamón, marisquito, oreja a la plancha o bikini de rabo de toro

restaurants.name: Caja de Cerillas
restaurants.signature_dish: Empanadillas rellenas de tomate y atún, albóndigas al jerez, judías verdes con patatas y jamón
restaurants.quote: Casa de comidas tradicional, de las de toda la vida. Estacionalidad absoluta del producto. Empanadillas rellenas de tomate y atún, albóndigas al jerez, judías verdes con patatas y jamón

restaurants.name: EMI
restaurants.quote: Propuesta de Autor con mayúscula, personalísima, intransferible, compleja —a veces, incluso demasiado—, técnicamente sobresaliente. Menú degustación de 14 pasos a 195 euros

restaurants.name: La Bechamel Madrid
restaurants.signature_dish: Croqueta (campeona Madrid Fusión 2023), gazpachos manchegos de caza, perdiz roja en escabeche
restaurants.quote: Hermano pequeño de Ababol, más desenfadado, más informal y más asequible. Croqueta que ganó el campeonato de Madrid Fusión 2023, pisto con patatas, gazpachos manchegos de caza, perdiz roja en escabeche, coliflor adobada con sésamo y raita

restaurants.name: La Embajada de Serrano
restaurants.signature_dish: Arroz con berberechos, merluza envuelta en alga nori con salsa meunière, canelón de pollo guisado con salsa de foie
restaurants.quote: Pincho de tortilla cremosa con cebolla confitada, rabas, arroz con berberechos, merluza envuelta en alga nori con salsa meunière, canelón de pollo guisado con salsa de foie

restaurants.name: La Mar
restaurants.signature_dish: Cebiches, tiraditos, piqueos
restaurants.quote: Espíritu festivo propio de las calles de Lima. Primera cevichería europea de Gastón Acurio, con piqueos, cebiches, tiraditos y platos de parrilla, y una carta de cócteles y piscos

restaurants.name: Makoto
restaurants.signature_dish: Nigiris
restaurants.quote: Adaptó la sensibilidad y los sabores de su país a los gustos y los paladares de los estadounidenses. Primera sede europea del chef Makoto Okuwa, especializada en nigiris y con talleres de sake

restaurants.name: Osaka Nikkei
restaurants.signature_dish: Langostinos en salsa de lulo, costillas glaseadas con miel de cítricos
restaurants.quote: Sashimis, tiraditos, nigiris, laminados y asados, langostinos en salsa de lulo, costillas glaseadas con miel de cítricos. Ocupa el espacio del primer gran restaurante japonés de España

restaurants.name: Ramón Freixa Atelier
restaurants.quote: Juguete que permite al chef sacar su vena más ácrata y provocadora: platos atrevidos, disfrutones y técnicamente apabullantes. Incondicionalmente por el mejor producto posible, tratado con mimo casi quirúrgico en busca de su esencia. Menú degustación a 230 euros para diez comensales

restaurants.name: Sen Omakase
restaurants.quote: Maestría en el arte del sushi y los nigiris. Formato íntimo para 12 comensales con puesta en escena elegante y sobria, cocina kaiseki distribuida en cuatro salas distintas

restaurants.name: Kyoshi Las Cortes
restaurants.signature_dish: Sashimi de toro con caviar
restaurants.quote: Fusión refinada entre la tradición japonesa y los sabores mediterráneos, con barra de sushi y platos como sashimi de toro con caviar, y selección de sakes y vinos

restaurants.name: Kabuki Madrid
restaurants.quote: Fusiona la tradición nipona con la gastronomía mediterránea a través de técnica precisa y la calidad del producto, en un ambiente elegante y sobrio

restaurants.name: Pabú
restaurants.quote: Cocina de inspiración francesa con producto español de máxima calidad. Todo se elabora a diario —pan, postres, helados— con respeto a las microtemporadas y ejecución transparente

restaurants.name: Haramboure
restaurants.signature_dish: Tarta tatín de cebollas de Zalla
restaurants.quote: Sencillez y el respeto al sabor original de los ingredientes, con verduras de Bizkaia y Aranjuez, pescados del Cantábrico y carnes selectas. Tarta tatín de cebollas de Zalla como ejemplo de autenticidad

restaurants.name: El Gran Asador Lecanda
restaurants.signature_dish: Chuletón de vaca rubia gallega, pulpo a la brasa
restaurants.quote: La brasa como protagonista, con chuletón de vaca rubia gallega y pulpo a la brasa, bodega excelente y servicio atento y profesional

restaurants.name: Amós
restaurants.signature_dish: Ravioli de centollo con crema de marisco, rodaballo al pilpil
restaurants.quote: Rescata los sabores del norte de España con una mirada refinada y actual. Ravioli de centollo con crema de marisco y rodaballo al pilpil entre sus platos más destacados

restaurants.name: Zalacaín
restaurants.signature_dish: Steak tartar preparado en sala, solomillo Wellington
restaurants.quote: Mítico templo gastronómico madrileño renovado sin perder su esencia clásica. Steak tartar preparado en sala y solomillo Wellington entre sus clásicos, combinando tradición con elegancia y técnica culinaria impecable

restaurants.name: VelascoAbellà
restaurants.quote: Cocina de mercado contemporánea que resalta la calidad del producto de temporada, con técnica y elegancia en cada plato, en un espacio de luminosidad y diseño cuidado

restaurants.name: Lana
restaurants.signature_dish: Empanadas de vacío a la brasa, mollejas de ternera
restaurants.quote: Parrilla argentina centrada en carnes de alta calidad —Aberdeen Angus, Wagyu y vacuno gallego— a la brasa, con empanadas de vacío a la brasa, mollejas de ternera y amplia selección de vinos argentinos

restaurants.name: Verdejo
restaurants.signature_dish: Guiso de pochas con codorniz, alcachofas confitadas con jamón ibérico
restaurants.quote: Honra el producto de temporada y la cocina de mercado con carta que cambia constantemente. Guiso de pochas con codorniz y alcachofas confitadas con jamón ibérico, con ambiente relajado y bodega cuidada

restaurants.name: La Tasquería
restaurants.signature_dish: Callos con pata y morro, canelón de oreja, tartar de lengua
restaurants.quote: Personalísima propuesta de casquería elevada a la alta cocina: callos con pata y morro, canelón de oreja y tartar de lengua. Javi Estévez transforma lo atrevido en arte culinario

restaurants.name: Kappo
restaurants.quote: Oda a la cocina kaiseki japonesa sin menú fijo, con platos que cambian según el producto disponible. Cocina en vivo ante el comensal en barra de madera minimalista, con precisión técnica y respeto absoluto por el producto

restaurants.name: Cebo
restaurants.signature_dish: Pichón con mole, sopa de cebolla reinventada
restaurants.quote: Experiencia de alta cocina con identidad propia basada en el territorio. Pichón con mole y sopa de cebolla reinventada. Black grouper, vaca de trabajo beef, and Andine Cornue tomatoes on the tasting menu

restaurants.name: Saddle
restaurants.signature_dish: Smoked eel and ibérico ham velouté, tartare of Mediterranean red prawn
restaurants.quote: Right now, the city's best restaurant bar none. Smoked eel and ibérico ham velouté, tartare of Mediterranean red prawn, and a refined modern take on tournedos Rossini. Service is finely honed and perfectly pitched

restaurants.name: La Tasquita de Enfrente
restaurants.signature_dish: Guisantes lágrima con ibérico papada
restaurants.quote: Guisantes lágrima—the season's first baby peas, cooked with a little ibérico papada (bacon)—is a luxury greater than caviar. Near-cult status in recent years, daily cooking with a limited number of ingredients

restaurants.name: Lhardy
restaurants.signature_dish: Sole cooked with champagne, consommé from a silver samovar with sherry, steak tartare
restaurants.quote: Historic and charming. Sole cooked with champagne, exemplary steak tartare, and consommé served from a silver samovar and spiked with a dash of sherry

restaurants.name: Casa Salvador
restaurants.signature_dish: Riñones al Jerez, gallina en pepitoria, rabo de toro
restaurants.quote: Atmospheric interiors crammed with tiles and bullfight memorabilia. Riñones al Jerez, gallina en pepitoria, and rabo de toro. Established 1941

restaurants.name: La Trainera
restaurants.signature_dish: Grilled turbot
restaurants.quote: Maritime-themed interior and fish-forward, Basque-inflected menu. Grilled turbot, often said to be the best in Madrid. Established 1966

restaurants.name: Sacha
restaurants.signature_dish: Tortilla vaga, false lasagne with crab, pickled oyster
restaurants.quote: Laidback atmosphere and idiosyncratic bistro menu. Tortilla vaga, false lasagne with crab, pickled oyster, and lentils. In summer the little terraza out back is one of Madrid's prettiest dining spots

restaurants.name: El Invernadero
name_variants: El Invernadero Rodrigo de la Calle
restaurants.quote: All vegetables, many from Rodrigo de la Calle's own farm in Aranjuez. Vegetalia tasting menu with fermented beverages made from vegetables and fruits alongside fine wines. Michelin star and Green Star

restaurants.name: OSA
restaurants.quote: Home-made charcuterie, an on-site smokery for game and fish, French techniques and a Japanese sensibility. Highly personal and, in the best sense, somewhat radical menu. Michelin star, 20 diners per service

restaurants.name: La Parra
restaurants.signature_dish: Steak tartare, roast suckling pig, black rice with prawns
restaurants.quote: Quirkily comfortable Anglo-Andalusian interior with a discreetly exclusive vibe. Steak tartare, roast suckling pig, and black rice with prawns. Notable clientele includes the Obamas

restaurants.name: DiverXO
restaurants.quote: Three Michelin stars. Described as a 'punk' restaurant and the most disruptive force on the city's gastronomic scene. Dabiz Muñoz—a genius, there's no one quite like him

restaurants.name: Ugo Chan
restaurants.signature_dish: Onion soup in smoked bonito consommé with Comté cheese
restaurants.quote: Japanese cuisine with Spanish flair, featuring an omakase tasting menu and the signature onion soup in smoked bonito consommé with Comté cheese, in a minimalist room with prominent bar and wood accents

restaurants.name: Desborre
restaurants.signature_dish: Anguiano bean stew (modernised)
restaurants.quote: Chef Lucía Grávalos from La Rioja brings a modestly sized yet charming space with a modernised traditional Anguiano bean stew. Tasting menu and à la carte available

restaurants.name: Per Sé Bistro
restaurants.quote: French-Basque-inspired cooking with Latin touches from chef Andrés Madriagal, who spent ten years in Panama. Earned a Repsol Sun in months after opening, formerly home to Arce restaurant for 35+ years

restaurants.name: Desde 1911
restaurants.quote: Seafood restaurant from Pescaderías Coruñesas with seasonal menu changes. Front-of-house Abel Valverde, National Gastronomy Award winner 2008, and head chef Diego Murciego. Extensive expertise backed by over a century of history

restaurants.name: Bascoat
restaurants.quote: Basque cuisine by Rodrigo García and Nagore Irazuegi, where tradition is refreshed with cosmopolitan touches. Moved from Arima in Ríos Rosas to a more upscale location. Earned two Repsol Soles

restaurants.name: Deessa
restaurants.quote: Two Michelin stars. Spectacular dining room with garden views and exquisite service at the Mandarin Oriental Ritz. Quique Dacosta's vision executed by head chef Guillermo Chávez, with two very different tasting menus

restaurants.name: Smoked Room
restaurants.quote: Two Michelin stars, awarded simultaneously—an unprecedented achievement. Smoke is the single most important element in hyper-stylised haute cuisine delivered as a dazzling show-cooking experience. Head chef Massimiliano Delle Vedove

restaurants.name: Corral de la Morería
restaurants.signature_dish: Squid noodles, pigeon, intxaursalsa
restaurants.quote: Legendary flamenco venue with the world's most important sherry cellar. Chef David García's Gargantúa tasting menu includes squid noodles, pigeon, and intxaursalsa. Michelin starred
restaurants.flag_note: Primarily a flamenco tablao show venue; Michelin-starred kitchen retained per user decision.

restaurants.name: Tripea
restaurants.signature_dish: Hot mussel ceviche prepared in a wok
restaurants.quote: Peruvian with global influences—Andes and Asia. Hot mussel ceviche prepared in a wok. Bar and communal table. Good food, good conversation, and positive vibe at Mercado de Vallehermoso

restaurants.name: Estimar
restaurants.quote: Shellfish temple with a purist approach—only three or four ingredients per dish. Rafa Zafra cleans just 20 anchovies in the morning and another 20 in the afternoon. Anna Gotanegra sources seafood from the Catalan coast

restaurants.name: La Bien Aparecida
restaurants.signature_dish: Porrusalda, grilled artichoke with breadcrumbs, oxtail stew
restaurants.quote: Nearly ten years on Serrano, focused on Cantabrian cuisine with emphasis on vegetables. Porrusalda, grilled artichoke with breadcrumbs, and oxtail stew. Chef José Manuel de Dios trained under Michel Bras, Joan Roca, and Pedro Subijana

restaurants.name: Lakasa
restaurants.signature_dish: Sirloin Wellington, verdina beans in pepitoria sauce, roasted skate
restaurants.quote: César Martín's sirloin Wellington (certain days only), verdina beans in pepitoria sauce, roasted skate, and lemon tart. Warm, reliable cooking with genuine seasonal character

restaurants.name: Trèsde
restaurants.signature_dish: Crispy potato chip layers with vegetables and mayonnaise
restaurants.quote: Three-course format with three options per section. Additional bite of crispy potato chip layers with vegetables and mayonnaise. Menu changes with the seasons, served without tablecloth

restaurants.name: Le Bistroman
restaurants.signature_dish: Pissaladière from Nice, escargots in herb butter, bouillabaisse
restaurants.quote: Possibly the best French restaurant in Madrid, transporting diners to Provence. Pissaladière from Nice, escargots in herb butter, French onion soup, and bouillabaisse. Exquisite pâté and boeuf bourguignon ravioli

restaurants.name: Noi
restaurants.signature_dish: Fried pizzetta with coppa, tagliatelle with tuna cheek ragù
restaurants.quote: Chef Gianni Pinto combines ingredients from across the Mediterranean—forget clichés and traditions. Fried pizzetta with coppa, caponata with charred aubergine emulsion, and tagliatelle with tuna cheek ragù

restaurants.name: Ikigai
restaurants.signature_dish: Chawan mushi, off-menu nigiri
restaurants.quote: Chawan mushi represents harmonious fusion, technical precision, and balanced flavours. Off-menu nigiri each week—warm versions are a must-try. High-quality ingredients and a relaxed atmosphere from chef Yong Wu Nagahira

restaurants.name: Tres por Cuatro
restaurants.signature_dish: Rossejat with skate and sobrasada, rabbit and snail stew
restaurants.quote: Chef Álex Marugán's concise, refined menu built on exceptional stocks and broths. Rossejat with skate and sobrasada, swordfish belly, and rabbit and snail stew. Wise and methodical elevation of seasonal ingredients

restaurants.name: El Sobrino de Botín
restaurants.signature_dish: Cochinillo asado, cordero asado
restaurants.quote: Probably Madrid's most famous restaurant—the world's oldest restaurant according to Guinness World Records, opened 1725. Suckling pig cooked in the original wood-fired oven, with underground wine caves

restaurants.name: El Ñeru
restaurants.signature_dish: Fabada asturiana
restaurants.quote: Famous for their fabada (Asturian bean stew) and excellent menú del día. One of the most authentic Asturian restaurants in Madrid, near the Puerta del Sol

restaurants.name: Casa Dani
restaurants.signature_dish: Spanish tortilla
restaurants.quote: One of my favorite tortillas in the city, inside Mercado de la Paz on Calle Ayala. Traditional dishes and a famous menú del día

restaurants.name: Celso y Manolo
restaurants.signature_dish: Beefsteak tomato salad, red prawns
restaurants.quote: One of my favorite restaurants in all of Madrid. Organic food, beefsteak tomato salad, and beautiful red prawns. Calle Libertad, Chueca

restaurants.name: El Cogollo de la Descarga
restaurants.signature_dish: Grilled octopus, fabada
restaurants.quote: THE place to try traditional northern Spanish food, steps away from Plaza Mayor. Grilled octopus and fabada among the highlights

restaurants.name: La Sanabresa
restaurants.signature_dish: Cocido Madrileño
restaurants.quote: A place trapped in time—a proper casa de comidas on Calle Amor de Dios serving cocido Madrileño and traditional home cooking at honest prices

restaurants.name: Tandoori Station
restaurants.quote: Hands down the best Indian restaurant in Madrid. Reservations far in advance recommended. On Calle José Ortega y Gasset in Salamanca

restaurants.name: La Rebonita
restaurants.signature_dish: Lamb cazuela, cucumber margarita with spicy rim
restaurants.quote: My favorite casual Mexican restaurant in town. Lamb cazuela and a cucumber margarita with spicy rim on Calle Olmo in Lavapiés

restaurants.name: Lambuzo
restaurants.signature_dish: Salmorejo, croquetas
restaurants.quote: Family-owned southern Spanish restaurant on Calle de las Conchas in La Latina. Salmorejo and croquetas among the highlights
